stories.title: **Headline:** Bedlam Softball Is Back — No. 1 Oklahoma Hosts Oklahoma State at Devon Park Tonight

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Bedlam Softball is TONIGHT. 🥎

No. 1 Oklahoma vs. No. 21 Oklahoma State
Devon Park, Oklahoma City | 8 PM CT | ESPN2

The same venue hosting the WCWS in May.
Kendall Wells on the field. 31 HRs. 6 from the all-time record.

This is must-watch softball. #Sooners #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Bedlam Softball is LIVE on ESPN2. 🥎

No. 1 Oklahoma vs. No. 21 Oklahoma State — Devon Park, OKC.

Kendall Wells steps in against a Cowgirl pitching staff that knows exactly what she can do.

Six from history. Every at-bat matters. #Sooners #CollegeSoftball

**Why it matters:** Bedlam on national TV with Wells' HR watch. Two of the top 25 programs in the sport. Devon Park hosts the WCWS in May — prime time showcase.

stories.title: **Headline:** Kendall Wells, 6 Away From History — Every At-Bat Is Must-Watch TV

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Lauren Espinoza set the all-time NCAA single-season HR record in 1995.

37 home runs. Arizona. It has stood for 31 years.

Kendall Wells has 31. She needs 6 more.

Oklahoma has 12+ games left. Do the math. #Sooners #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Just to frame tonight's Bedlam game:

Kendall Wells is batting .383 with 60 RBIs.
She leads the nation in home runs.
She's a freshman. 🥎

If she goes yard on ESPN2 tonight, that's HR No. 32 toward an all-time record.

No pressure or anything. #Sooners #CollegeSoftball

research.facts:
Kendall Wells All-Time HR Chase
**Status:** FOLLOW UP (April 14 — Wells at 31 HRs)
**Tier:** 1
**Sources:**
- soonersports.com (April 12, 2026): Wells hit 31st HR vs. Texas — "Wells Sets NCAA Freshman Home Run Record in 4-3 Win at No. 4 Texas"
- justwomenssports.com (2026): "Oklahoma Softball Star Kendall Wells Breaks SEC Home Run Record" — also confirmed 31 HRs, .383 BA, 60 RBIs, leads nation in HRs
- oudaily.com (April 12, 2026): "OU softball's Kendall Wells blasts 31st vs. Texas, breaks freshman single-season home run record"
- NiJaree Canady Wikipedia page (for reference on all-time record holder): Lauren Espinoza, Arizona, 37 HRs, 1995
**Key Facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources):**
- Kendall Wells current HR total: 31
- Previous NCAA freshman record: 30 (shared by Lauren Chamberlain, Jocelyn Alo, Kelly Majam)
- All-time NCAA single-season record: 37 by Lauren Espinoza, Arizona, 1995
- Wells HRs needed to break all-time record: 6
- Wells batting average: .383
- Wells RBIs: 60
- Wells leads nation in home runs
- Oklahoma has 12+ regular season games remaining
**Freshness:** Story continues from April 14 coverage. Every game is a new development. Tonight's Bedlam on ESPN2 is the next chapter.

stories.title: **Headline:** Virginia Tech Is a Legit WCWS Contender Now — The Hokies Are Here

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Virginia Tech is 35-6, 10-4 in the ACC, and ranked No. 12.

They beat No. 21 Virginia 5-2 last weekend.
They're in the national seed conversation.

Nobody saw this coming in February. The Hokies are a WCWS contender. 🥎 #CollegeSoftball

stories.title: **Headline:** 2026 WCWS Goes to 32 Teams — What the Expanded Field Means for the Race to OKC

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
The 2026 WCWS expands to a 32-team field. 🥎

More bids. More paths to Oklahoma City.
Devon Park. May 28–June 4/5.

Bubble teams like Stanford, Virginia Tech, and Duke now have more room.

The race to OKC just opened up. #CollegeSoftball #WCWS

research.facts:
2026 WCWS Expands to 32 Teams
**Status:** NEW STORY
**Tier:** 2
**Sources:**
- si.com/onsi/softball: "SEC, ACC Lead WCWS Field in First Softball America Bracketology Projection" — confirmed expanded field
- visitokc.com: "2026 NCAA Women's College World Series" — Devon Park, May 28-June 4/5
- ncaa.com/championships/softball/d1: Confirmed format change
**Key Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- 2026 WCWS location: Devon Park, Oklahoma City
- Dates: May 28 - June 4/5, 2026
- Field size: 32 teams (expanded from previous format)
- New seeding format: "buckets" to prevent early conference matchups
- Conferences with most projected hosts: SEC (most), then ACC (3), Big 12, Big Ten
**Freshness:** This is the 2026 tournament format — directly relevant throughout the season. The "expanded field" angle gives it fresh news value in April.

stories.title: **Headline:** Duke's Hot Streak Making the ACC Race Wider Than Ever

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
Duke softball has won 19 of its last 21 games.

15-3 in the ACC. Swept No. 17 Virginia. Wins over Virginia Tech and Stanford this season.

The Blue Devils are quietly one of the best teams in the country. 🥎

The ACC race is nothing like the preseason predicted. #CollegeSoftball
